data.text: Walmart agrees to pay $100 million to settle deceptive earnings charges, FTC says
data.snippet: Walmart has agreed to pay $100 million to settle charges that the company caused delivery drivers to lose tens of millions of dollars in earnings, the U.S. Federal Trade Commission said in a statement on Thursday.

image.text: A view shows the logo of a Walmart store in Ciudad Juarez, Mexico June 24, 2024.

data.text: Musk touts California robotaxis but Tesla does nothing to get permits
data.snippet: For more than a year, Elon Musk has repeatedly said Tesla is months away from launching a driverless robotaxi service in California – once state regulators give their blessing.

image.text: A Tesla robotaxi drives on the street along South Congress Avenue in Austin, Texas, U.S., June 22, 2025.

relatedHeadlines.text: Tesla starts robotaxi rides without safety monitors in Austin
relatedHeadlines.snippet:
Tesla CEO Elon Musk said on
Thursday that the company has started Robotaxi rides in Austin
without safety monitors in the car.

 (Reporting by Akash Sriram in Bengaluru; Editing by Alan
Barona)
 ((Akash.Sriram@thomsonreuters.com; On X as @HoodieOnVeshti;

relatedHeadlines.text: New York Governor Hochul pulls robotaxi pilot expansion proposal

data.text: India revamps mutual fund rulebook, opens room for higher gold and silver exposure
data.snippet: India's markets regulator on Thursday overhauled rules for mutual funds, introducing newer categories of schemes, curbing portfolio overlaps and allowing more exposure to gold and silver.

image.text: A general view of the the Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) headquarters in Mumbai, India, September 12, 2025.

relatedHeadlines.text: India markets regulator changes gold, silver valuation for mutual funds
relatedHeadlines.snippet: India's markets regulator on Thursday directed mutual funds to use domestic stock exchange spot prices to value their physical gold and silver holdings from April 1, 2026.

relatedHeadlines.text: India markets regulator revises mutual fund fees, defers conflict framework decision
relatedHeadlines.snippet: India's markets regulator on Wednesday approved changes to mutual fund fee structures to provide more transparency to investors, but postponed a decision on a new framework aimed at addressing conflicts of interest among its senior officials.

data.text: Poland's competition watchdog searches Allegro offices in Poznan, Warsaw
data.snippet: Poland's antimonopoly office UOKiK said on Thursday it had searched the headquarters of e-commerce platform Allegro in Poznan and its offices in Warsaw as part of explanatory proceedings.

image.text: Packages move through the Polish marketplace Allegro (ALE.WA) distribution centre, ahead of Christmas, in Adamow, Poland, December 11, 2024.

data.text: Italy softens penalties on irregularities by financial firms
data.snippet: Italy said on Thursday it would adopt a less punitive approach to irregularities by financial companies, in an attempt to improve the way its capital markets work and boost the appeal of the Milan bourse.

data.text: US closes probe into 7.4 million Stellantis vehicles without seeking recall
data.snippet: The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration on Thursday closed a long-running probe into nearly 7.4 million U.S. vehicles built by Chrysler-parent Stellantis over inadvertent deployments of active head rests without seeking a recall.

image.text: The logo of Stellantis is seen during the Automotive Industry Day summit in Paris, France, November 4, 2025.

data.text: UK's Serious Fraud Office appoints interim boss
data.snippet: Britain's Serious Fraud Office said on Thursday that Graham McNulty would take over in early April as interim director to lead the investigator and prosecutor.

data.text: EU top court rejects Air France, others' fight against EU cargo cartel fine
data.snippet: Air France KLM, British Airways and their peers on Thursday lost their fight against EU cartel fines totalling 776 million euros ($916 million), ending a more than two-decade old saga.

image.text: The logo of Air France-KLM Group is seen during the Air France-KLM Group's Annual Results 2025 press conference in Paris, France, February 19, 2026.

data.text: EXCLUSIVE-Google to test changes to search results, source says as EU fine looms
data.snippet: Alphabet's Google is poised to start testing changes to its search results to give rivals more prominence, a person with direct knowledge of the matter said on Wednesday, seeking to avoid an EU fine for allegedly favouring its own services in searches for hotels, flights and restaurants.

image.text: The Google logo is seen during Kering Innovation Day 2026 at the company?s headquarters in Paris, France, January 22, 2026.

data.text: FCC reviews growing shift of live sports to pay TV, subscription services
data.snippet: The Federal Communications Commission said on Wednesday it is reviewing the growing shift of live sports to pay TV and subscription services away from broadcast networks.

image.text: United States Federal Communications Commission logo and U.S. flag are seen in this illustration taken April 23, 2025.

data.text: Apple and Amazon took too long to remove anti-competitive clauses, Spanish watchdog says
data.snippet: Apple and Amazon took too long to take out anti-competitive clauses outlining Amazon's conditions as an Apple distributor that the companies had been told to remove immediately, Spain's competition regulator ruled on Wednesday.

image.text: A delivery man pushes an Amazon cart, along a street in Ronda, Spain January 28, 2025.

data.text: FAA issues directive to address Boeing 737 circuit breaker issue
data.snippet: The Federal Aviation Administration said on Wednesday it has issued an airworthiness directive for all Boeing 737 MAX 8 and 8200 airplanes to address a circuit breaker issue that could lead to a malfunction causing excessively high temperatures in the cabin and flight deck.

image.text: The logo of Boeing is seen at the 55th International Paris Airshow at Le Bourget Airport near Paris, France, June 20, 2025.

relatedHeadlines.text: Senator urges US House to take new vote on aviation safety bill
relatedHeadlines.snippet: The top Democrat on the U.S. Senate Commerce Committee on Wednesday urged the House of Representatives to reconsider aviation legislation that failed by one vote after Pentagon opposition.

data.text: South Korea watchdog fines Coupang $1.6 million for pressuring suppliers, delaying payments
data.snippet: South Korea's antitrust regulator said on Thursday it has fined Coupang 2.2 billion won ($1.55 million) for pressuring suppliers to cut prices and shoulder additional costs to meet profit margins, as well as for delaying payments to vendors.

image.text: Coupang logo is seen in this illustration taken February 11, 2025.

relatedHeadlines.text: BREAKINGVIEWS-Coupang investors brazenly channel Trump in Korea
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Silicon Valley's venture capitalists are taking a page out of President Donald Trump's playbook. Greenoaks Capital Partners and Altimeter Capital Management are asking the U.S. government to punish Seoul for what they say is unfair and discriminatory treatment of the $37 billion New York-listed Coupang following a data breach. It's a brazenly opportunistic test of bilateral ties, and if successful, might embolden others to capitalise on Washington's growing spread of diplomatic tensions.
